entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop being evaluated for resistance to pod borer

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The pod borer pest that attacks chickpea leaves, flowers, and pods

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea cultivars to withstand attack by Helicoverpa armigera

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The weight of grain produced per unit area, measured in kg/ha

entities.name: POD DAMAGE
entities.description: The percentage of pods damaged by Helicoverpa armigera

entities.name: EGG POPULATION
entities.description: The number of eggs laid by Helicoverpa armigera on the crop

entities.name: LARVAL POPULATION
entities.description: The number of larvae of Helicoverpa armigera present on the crop

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating multiple cultivars to identify those with specific traits

entities.name: DAMAGED RATING SCALE
entities.description: A scale used to categorize the level of damage caused by Helicoverpa armigera

entities.name: IPM PROGRAMME
entities.description: Integrated Pest Management program for controlling pests at a large farm level

entities.name: UNPROTECTED CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environmental setting where cultivars are exposed to pests without artificial protection

relationships.description: Screening was conducted on elite chickpea cultivars
Screening was used to evaluate resistance to this pest

The pest causes damage to the pods of the crop
The population of eggs produced by the pest on the crop
The population of larvae produced by the pest on the crop
The productivity of the crop measured by grain yield
The trait being evaluated in chickpea cultivars against the pest

The program used to manage the pest population
